rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with proper labeling and expression structure.
Generate valid AGREE contracts within annex agree blocks using type-scoped visible identifiers.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features at that scope.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Ensure all AGREE expressions use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and resolved identifiers.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Equations are declared as `eq name : type = expression;`.
Constants are declared as `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths in component type contracts.
Do not use AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
Do not invent new identifiers; only use visible features from the current component scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
Model fusion and architecture insertion locations
